item.title: Path traversal risk in web dashboard /api/init deployDir input
item.intent: deployDir submitted via web form must be sanitized and resolved to prevent writing files outside the user's home directory. No path traversal from web init.
item.description: src/web/server.tsx:240-245 accepts a user-supplied deployDir via POST /api/init and resolves ~ with a simple string replace, but does not validate the resulting path with path.resolve() or check it stays within the user's home directory. A crafted deployDir value like '../../sensitive' could cause agent config files to be written outside ~/.clawhq/. The web dashboard runs on localhost:3737 so this is local-only, but it is still a path traversal vulnerability. Steps: (1) import { resolve } from 'path' in server.tsx; (2) after resolving ~, apply path.resolve() to canonicalize; (3) validate the resolved path starts with homedir() — if not, reject with 400 error; (4) run web/ tests to confirm no regression. Scope: ~5 lines in one file.
